lessons.title: Welcome and Mindset
lessons.description: Learn the BMH Group service standard and the mindset that keeps seller conversations clear, calm, and human.

content.html:
What you will learn

Learn the BMH Group service standard and the mindset that keeps seller conversations clear, calm, and human.

  • Explain the BMH service standard
  • Put service before pressure
  • Use curiosity and clarity in seller conversations
  • Detach from outcomes while staying accountable

Learner guide

  • The goal is an aligned decision, not a forced yes
  • Listen for the seller's real problem before discussing a solution
  • Treat repetition as practice that creates calm
  • A respectful no is better than a pressured agreement

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

quiz.title: Welcome and Mindset Checkpoint
quiz.description: Each attempt draws 10 questions from the curated lesson pool.

questions.question_text: Beyond the day-to-day job, what is the BMH Group's mission?
questions.explanation: Andrea defines the mission as helping families overcome difficult situations and walk away with clarity, certainty, and the ability to move forward.

options.option_text: Helping families overcome difficult situations and move forward with clarity and certainty
Closing more cash deals per month than any competitor
Building the largest rental portfolio in the region
Teaching homeowners to list their own properties

questions.question_text: What is the core 'tool' BMH uses to give homeowners speed and certainty?
questions.explanation: BMH buys houses for cash, as-is, so a homeowner can move on without repairs, agents, or months of waiting - the training is about how you use that tool.

options.option_text: Listing homes on the MLS at a discount
Providing free repairs before the sale
Arranging traditional mortgage financing for buyers

questions.question_text: What does the script give a rep? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The script is a valuable tool that provides structure, confidence, and a clear path through every conversation - but it isn't what makes a rep exceptional.

options.option_text: A clear path through every conversation
A guarantee the seller will say yes
The exact dollar amount to offer

questions.question_text: What is the most powerful skill a rep can develop?
questions.explanation: The most powerful thing you can develop is the ability to lead a homeowner to the best path for their situation - their path, not necessarily our company's.

options.option_text: Leading a homeowner to the best possible path for their situation - even if it isn't BMH's path
Calculating a maximum allowable offer in their head
Delivering the cash offer within the first five minutes

questions.question_text: Under the 'guide, not buyer' mindset, what should a rep focus on winning?
questions.explanation: When you stop trying to win the property and start winning clarity for the seller, the whole dynamic shifts.

options.option_text: Clarity for the seller, not the property
The argument over the home's value
A same-day signature on the agreement

questions.question_text: What does opening with the seller's problem - instead of property specs - accomplish? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Leading with their problem, not the property specs, is how you earn trust and get to real motivation fast.

options.option_text: It gets to real motivation fast
It guarantees a signed contract on the first call

questions.question_text: In the inherited-house example, what happens the moment a rep stops chasing the deal and starts solving the seller's problem?
questions.explanation: The seller who inherited a house two states away wants simple, not top dollar - solving her problem disarms her defenses and she starts talking.

options.option_text: She stops guarding and starts talking
She immediately accepts the first cash offer
She insists on listing with an agent instead
She stops returning calls until the offer increases

questions.question_text: Acknowledging a seller's specific burden - like a problem tenant - before talking numbers often makes them feel truly heard for the first time, and that is when conversations turn into deals.
questions.explanation: When you acknowledge the burden before the numbers, you're usually the first person who actually heard them - and that's when conversations turn into deals.

questions.question_text: Which questions help a seller 'write the movie' of life after the sale? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Helping a frozen seller picture the end - where they'll live after closing and how much cash is in their pocket - turns the deal into a natural next step instead of a hard close.

options.option_text: 'How much cash is in your pocket?'

questions.question_text: What happens when a rep gets too attached to the outcome of a deal? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: If you feel needy you'll sound needy, and desperation drives sellers away - staying detached is what keeps the tone professional and the status equal.

options.option_text: They start to sound needy or desperate
Sellers lose respect and trust breaks
They become better listeners under pressure
Sellers sign faster because of the urgency

questions.question_text: If a rep asks a simple question and then stays silent, sellers will often reveal the real reason they're selling.
questions.explanation: Sellers tell you the real reason when you give them room - most people never get that room because the other person keeps filling the silence.

questions.question_text: A seller's number doesn't work for BMH. What should the rep do?
questions.explanation: Equal status means saying it plainly while keeping rapport intact - sellers respect directness and can't stand desperation or dismissiveness.

options.option_text: Say so plainly while keeping rapport intact
Accept the number and renegotiate later
Mark the lead dead and move on the same day
Dodge the price question until the next call

questions.question_text: According to Principle 7, you don't overcome people - you outlast confusion.
questions.explanation: Principle 7, 'Long Game Wins,' is about outlasting confusion rather than overcoming people.

questions.question_text: What should long-term follow-up with a high-price seller look like? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Follow up with warmth and bring a new angle each time - time matures motivation, and the relationship keeps BMH first in line when the seller is finally ready.

options.option_text: A new angle each time you reach out
The identical pitch repeated until they cave

questions.question_text: You should be willing to be bad on call one so you can be great on call one thousand.
questions.explanation: Reps create calm - the edge builds by making the extra call, not by expecting perfection on day one.

questions.question_text: Where does real confidence on calls come from?
questions.explanation: Principle 9 says confidence comes from thousands of conversations, not hype.

options.option_text: Watching recordings of top closers every week

questions.question_text: What is a seller's 'decision curve'?
questions.explanation: Most high-price sellers are early in their decision curve - time and warm follow-up mature their motivation until they're ready to accept an offer.

options.option_text: The timeline their motivation follows from first contact to being ready to accept an offer
The formula for the wholesale offer percentage
A chart of the listing's days on market
The scripted order of discovery questions

questions.question_text: According to Andrea, what stays with you long after the call ends and the deal closes?
questions.explanation: Andrea calls it fulfilling, real work - the kind that stays with you long after the call ends.

options.option_text: The fulfillment of helping families through hard moments
The size of the assignment fee

lessons.title: Real Estate Terms Glossary
lessons.description: Build the vocabulary needed to follow property, title, financing, and transaction conversations without guessing.

content.html:
What you will learn

Build the vocabulary needed to follow property, title, financing, and transaction conversations without guessing.

  • Define distressed, off-market, on-market, MLS, and listing terms
  • Distinguish wholesaling, assignment, and double-close concepts
  • Recognize subject-to and seller-financing structures
  • Ask for clarification when a term affects a seller

Learner guide

  • Terms are tools for understanding, not jargon to impress sellers
  • Translate technical language into plain English
  • Never guess about legal or title questions
  • Use the current deal record as the source for property facts

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

content.description: Accessible learner guide for Real Estate Terms Glossary

cards.front: A home in rough physical shape - or owned by someone going through a hard life situation - is called what?
cards.back: A distressed property

cards.front: Which of these describe an off-market property? (Select all that apply)
cards.back: Not publicly listed for sale anywhere; No real estate agent involved; The owner may not have even decided to sell yet

cards.front: An expired listing - a property that was listed but never sold - can be a great lead because the seller is often frustrated and more motivated.
cards.back: True

cards.front: A house that has sat on the MLS for 200 days is a sign the seller may be getting desperate to get rid of it.
cards.back: True

cards.front: Transferring your rights in a purchase agreement to another buyer is called what?
cards.back: An assignment of contract

cards.front: In seller financing, the seller acts as the bank and receives payments directly from the buyer over time.
cards.back: True

cards.front: A Notice of Default is the formal legal document that officially starts the foreclosure process.
cards.back: True

cards.front: An absentee owner is someone who lives full-time in the property they own.
cards.back: False

quiz.title: Real Estate Terms Glossary Checkpoint
quiz.description: Each attempt draws 10 questions from the curated lesson pool.

questions.question_text: A home in rough physical shape - or owned by someone going through a hard life situation - is called what?
questions.explanation: A distressed property is one in rough shape or owned by someone in a hard situation, like the broken-down car on the roadside that won't sell at full price.

questions.question_text: Which of these describe an off-market property? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Off-market is BMH's sweet spot - reaching homeowners before they ever consider the traditional route, like a reservation before the restaurant opens to the public.

options.option_text: Not publicly listed for sale anywhere
The owner may not have even decided to sell yet
It shows up on Zillow with a price cut
It has an expired MLS listing

questions.question_text: An expired listing - a property that was listed but never sold - can be a great lead because the seller is often frustrated and more motivated.
questions.explanation: The traditional route already failed these sellers, which makes them very open to a direct cash offer.

questions.question_text: A house that has sat on the MLS for 200 days is a sign the seller may be getting desperate to get rid of it.
questions.explanation: High DOM is a motivation signal - the longer a property sits without selling, the more desperate the seller is likely to be.

questions.question_text: Transferring your rights in a purchase agreement to another buyer is called what?
questions.explanation: In an assignment of contract, the end buyer closes on the property instead of BMH, and BMH collects an assignment fee for putting the deal together.

questions.question_text: In seller financing, the seller acts as the bank and receives payments directly from the buyer over time.
questions.explanation: You likely won't negotiate these on calls, but knowing they exist lets you confidently say the team has several ways to structure a deal.

questions.question_text: A Notice of Default is the formal legal document that officially starts the foreclosure process.
questions.explanation: It's the lender putting 'we've been patient enough' in writing - flag any seller who mentions an official notice for your acquisition manager immediately.

questions.question_text: An absentee owner is someone who lives full-time in the property they own.
questions.explanation: It's the opposite - absentee owners don't live in the property, and many are long-distance landlords tired of paying for a place they never visit.

questions.question_text: ARV is the estimated value of a property in its current, un-renovated condition.
questions.explanation: ARV is the After Repair Value - what the property would be worth after full renovation, and the starting point for all offer calculations.

questions.question_text: Which items get subtracted from ARV to calculate the MAO? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: MAO starts at the ARV and works backwards - repairs, profit margin, and closing costs come off the top, like the $12,000 fixer-upper car example.

questions.question_text: The higher a property's repair estimate, the lower BMH's offer needs to be.
questions.explanation: Like a car needing engine, transmission, and body work, heavy rehab costs push the offer down.

questions.question_text: Which statements describe the earnest money deposit (EMD)? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The EMD is skin in the game - like a deposit to hold a venue - and it sits with the title company, not the seller, until closing.

options.option_text: A small good-faith payment made when the purchase agreement is signed
It shows the buyer intends to follow through
It's held by the title company until closing
It's BMH's profit on the wholesale deal
It's handed directly to the seller in cash

questions.question_text: A legal claim against a property for an unpaid debt - back taxes, contractor bills, judgments - is called what?
questions.explanation: Like a hold on a bank account, a lien must be paid off or settled before the property can sell cleanly.

questions.question_text: What is 'the pipeline'?
questions.explanation: The pipeline is managed in Sandra and works like a funnel - lots of leads go in at the top and a percentage make it all the way to a closed deal.

options.option_text: Every active lead and deal, at every stage from first contact through closing
The list of end buyers waiting for the next contract
The day's list of scheduled callbacks

questions.question_text: Which statements describe end buyers? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: End buyers are the other side of the wholesale equation - they need deals, BMH finds deals.

options.option_text: Investors who buy properties from wholesalers like BMH
They renovate the property to resell or rent it
They need deals - and BMH finds deals
They act as the neutral third party at closing
They list BMH's contracts on the MLS

questions.question_text: What is Sandra?
questions.explanation: Sandra is the brain of the operation - every lead, contact, note, and follow-up task lives there, and if it's not in Sandra, it didn't happen.

options.option_text: The CRM where every lead, contact, note, and follow-up task lives
The property research tool used to estimate values
The database agents use to list homes for sale

questions.question_text: What is the goal of every early-stage call?
questions.explanation: You're not there to make the offer or close the deal - a scheduled next step with the acquisition manager is the win.

options.option_text: Set an appointment for the acquisition manager to have a deeper conversation
Deliver a full cash offer with exact numbers
Get the purchase agreement signed on the phone
Walk the seller through the ARV math

questions.question_text: Which of these are call dispositions you'd record in Sandra after a dial? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: A disposition is the recorded outcome of a call - accurate dispositions keep the pipeline clean and get the right leads the right attention.

lessons.description: Understand where lead data, calls, research, practice, time tracking, and team communication belong.

content.html:
What you will learn

Understand where lead data, calls, research, practice, time tracking, and team communication belong.

  • Identify the purpose of each core tool
  • Keep Sandra as the lead source of truth
  • Use Closer Lab for deliberate practice
  • Escalate blocked work through the correct team channel

Learner guide

  • If lead activity is not recorded in Sandra it is not operationally visible
  • Use property tools for research and Sandra for relationship history
  • Complete assigned tasks and next actions on time
  • Ask your manager for the current SOP when a tool workflow changes

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

quiz.title: Tech Stack and Systems Checkpoint
quiz.description: Each attempt draws 10 questions from the curated lesson pool.

questions.question_text: Which application serves as the central CRM and "single source of truth" for the BMH Group?
questions.explanation: Sandra is the CRM at the center of everything - every lead, conversation, and deal lives inside it.

questions.question_text: After a conversation with a homeowner, you log your notes in Sandra.
questions.explanation: Every homeowner conversation gets its notes logged in Sandra.

questions.question_text: Where do the daily tasks, follow-ups, and process checklists your manager assigns live?
questions.explanation: Tasks live in Sandra too - assignments, follow-ups, and process checklists are all found there.

questions.question_text: Which homeowner and property categories does the team pull from PropStream? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: PropStream lists include owners behind on taxes, probate situations, code violations, and absentee owners.

options.option_text: Properties with code violations or absentee owners
Homeowners who just listed with a top agent

questions.question_text: What is the functional difference between PropStream and Sandra in the lead pipeline?
questions.explanation: PropStream is where leads are found in the first place; Sandra is where the resulting relationships are managed.

options.option_text: PropStream finds the leads; Sandra manages the relationships
PropStream records calls; Sandra sends the offers
PropStream tracks hours; Sandra is the phone system
PropStream is the training platform; Sandra stores SOPs

questions.question_text: When driving for dollars, which property conditions are you looking to flag in DealMachine? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: DealMachine is used to flag properties that look vacant, neglected, or distressed.

options.option_text: Properties that look neglected or distressed
Freshly renovated homes with staged photos

questions.question_text: What is the primary role of Deal Sniper in the BMH workflow?
questions.explanation: Deal Sniper is used to generate offers quickly and reliably so the team can make confident offers without wasting time.

options.option_text: Tracking hours and activity for payroll

questions.question_text: Which platform do all outbound phone calls to homeowners go through?
questions.explanation: DialPad is the phone system - all outbound homeowner calls go through it and are recorded and logged.

questions.question_text: Which call metrics can managers see through DialPad? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: DialPad shows managers how many calls you made and how long you were on the phone.

options.option_text: How long you were on the phone
Your quiz scores from the BMH Institute

questions.question_text: What is Closer Lab?
questions.explanation: Closer Lab is the AI speech coaching tool used specifically for training and role play before you talk to real homeowners.

options.option_text: An AI speech coaching tool used for training and role play
The phone system used for homeowner calls
The training platform where onboarding modules live
A tool for pulling lists of distressed owners

questions.question_text: What do you complete in Closer Lab before ever getting on the phone with a real homeowner? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: Before live calls, you work through objection role plays and call simulations with the AI roleplay agent.

options.option_text: Call simulations with the AI agent
Ten live calls to real distressed sellers

questions.question_text: Which tool tracks your hours and activity so you're paid accurately for the time you put in?
questions.explanation: HubStaff is the time-tracking tool - it tracks hours and activity so pay is accurate.

questions.question_text: What is the primary internal communication tool at BMH Group?
questions.explanation: Slack is how the team communicates - if you need to reach your manager, you do it in Slack.

questions.question_text: What is expected of you on Slack? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: Keep notifications on and stay active - Slack is where wins, updates, and team announcements are shared.

options.option_text: Stay active so you see wins, updates, and team announcements
Check it once a week during downtime

questions.question_text: How should you work through the modules in the BMH Institute?
questions.explanation: Work through the modules in order because each lesson builds on the one before it.

options.option_text: In order, because each lesson builds on the one before it
In any order - the modules are independent
Only the modules your manager specifically flags
Start from the final quiz and work backwards

questions.question_text: You can't find a specific SOP. Who do you ask?
questions.explanation: The lesson says to ask your team lead how to locate specific SOPs.

options.option_text: The AI agent in Closer Lab
Nobody - keep searching until you find it

questions.question_text: What does the BMH Institute track for your manager? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: The Institute tracks your module progress, with quizzes to make sure the material is sticking.

questions.question_text: You just got a new lead. What's the first thing you do with it?
questions.explanation: New leads go into Sandra first - that's how it stays the single source of truth.

options.option_text: Send an offer through Deal Sniper right away
Post it in Slack for the team to see
Add it to a Google Doc tracker

lessons.title: Humanizing the Lead
lessons.description: Recognize the people and pressures behind distressed-property data and determine whether BMH Group can genuinely help.

content.html:
What you will learn

Recognize the people and pressures behind distressed-property data and determine whether BMH Group can genuinely help.

  • Describe the ideal seller profile
  • Identify urgency, condition, equity, and decision-maker signals
  • Use respectful first-person language
  • Disqualify poor fits without dehumanizing the seller

Learner guide

  • A lead is a person in a real situation
  • Motivation is discovered through listening, not assumed from a list
  • Hard filters protect both the seller and the team
  • A seller's timeline and desired outcome matter as much as property data

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

quiz.title: Humanizing the Lead Checkpoint
quiz.description: Each attempt draws 10 questions from the curated lesson pool.

questions.question_text: How should leads in a CRM be viewed beyond being simple numbers?
questions.explanation: Each lead represents a real person dealing with real challenges in their life.

options.option_text: As real people experiencing real-life problems
As entries to be categorized and scored

questions.question_text: Why do heirs often avoid managing an inherited property themselves?
questions.explanation: Heirs face multiple practical barriers that make DIY management unappealing.

options.option_text: They lack the desire to manage tenants
They don't want to deal with contractors
They don't want to travel for repairs
They are legally prohibited from managing it
They prefer to let it appreciate in value

questions.question_text: What is a 'Tired Landlord'?
questions.explanation: This is a specific lead type that BMH Group frequently encounters.

options.option_text: A landlord who owns too many properties
A landlord who is retiring from real estate
A property owner frustrated by bad tenants, maintenance issues, and financial losses
A landlord who has been in the business for over 20 years

questions.question_text: How do life events like divorce or job relocation impact a seller's needs?
questions.explanation: Major life changes create specific demands around timing and ease of the sale process.

options.option_text: They create a need for simplicity
They create a need for speed
They create a need for urgency
They make the seller want top dollar

questions.question_text: Why is it dangerous to spend too much time with unmotivated sellers?
questions.explanation: Time is a valuable resource, and unmotivated sellers will not convert to deals.

options.option_text: They may file complaints against the company
They will share negative reviews online
They will waste the caller's time because they have no real pain or reason to accept a discount
They often provide inaccurate property information

questions.question_text: Under what condition should a seller be referred to a real estate agent instead of an investor?
questions.explanation: Sellers with pristine homes wanting full price are better served by the traditional market.

options.option_text: When the seller is rude on the phone
When the property is in another state
If the seller wants full retail value for a pristine house
When the seller has already spoken to other investors

questions.question_text: Which two property categories are explicitly excluded from the company's focus?
questions.explanation: BMH Group focuses on residential properties, not commercial or vacant land.

questions.question_text: What is the core element that starts the trust-building process?
questions.explanation: Trust begins before the first word is spoken, through research and preparation.

options.option_text: Understanding the seller and their situation before the initial hello

questions.question_text: A 'Tired Landlord' has often reached a point where they have _____.
questions.explanation: This phrase describes the emotional breaking point landlords reach.

questions.question_text: What does the phrase 'my tenants just trashed the place' signify to an investor?
questions.explanation: This phrase reveals a landlord who has reached their breaking point.

options.option_text: A low-value property not worth pursuing
A high level of motivation from a tired landlord
A property that needs to be condemned

questions.question_text: What should you do if you realize there is no equity in a property?
questions.explanation: Negative equity situations fall outside the company's typical deal structure.

options.option_text: Offer a lower price to compensate
Suggest the seller get a second mortgage
Recognize it as a situation where the company likely cannot help

questions.question_text: What is the primary motivation for an out-of-state owner of a vacant property?
questions.explanation: Vacant out-of-state properties are pure liability with no income offset.

options.option_text: Cashing out to avoid the headache of managing it from afar
Waiting for the market to improve

questions.question_text: When a seller says, 'I've had it listed and it's not selling,' what type of motivation does this signal?
questions.explanation: A failed listing creates frustration and urgency that wasn't there before.

options.option_text: Urgency caused by a failed traditional sale process

questions.question_text: Why is it important to distinguish motivation from curiosity quickly?
questions.explanation: Efficient qualification prevents wasting time on leads that won't convert.

options.option_text: Curious sellers are difficult to work with
To avoid wasting time on leads that will not result in a deal
Curious sellers often leave negative reviews

questions.question_text: If a house is sitting vacant and the owner lives elsewhere, their main goal is usually to _____.
questions.explanation: Vacant out-of-state properties represent pure liability that owners want to convert to cash.

questions.question_text: What creates the 'hostility' that callers often face from leads?
questions.explanation: Being bombarded with calls makes sellers defensive and frustrated.

options.option_text: The lead being hit by five to ten calls a day from various investors
Legal advice telling them not to sell

questions.question_text: Why is 'simplicity' a key selling point for those facing major life events?
questions.explanation: People in crisis cannot handle additional complexity in their lives.

options.option_text: Simple deals close faster for the company
Simplicity is a legal requirement for distressed sellers
These sellers are already dealing with high complexity and stress
Simple transactions have fewer compliance requirements

questions.question_text: What is the final goal of the module's teaching regarding seller interactions?
questions.explanation: Humanizing every interaction is the overarching theme of the training.

options.option_text: To maximize the number of deals closed
To minimize time spent on each call

lessons.title: The BMH Offer Playbook
lessons.description: Explain how a direct property purchase exchanges maximum retail price for speed, certainty, convenience, and an as-is sale.

content.html:
What you will learn

Explain how a direct property purchase exchanges maximum retail price for speed, certainty, convenience, and an as-is sale.

  • Explain the four offer pillars
  • Describe ARV and repair-cost inputs
  • Compare direct sale and traditional listing tradeoffs
  • Present the offer without promising an outcome

Learner guide

  • The offer is a solution with tradeoffs, not a claim to be the highest price
  • Use plain English when explaining the math
  • Confirm the seller values the benefits before positioning them
  • Do not invent property values or repair estimates

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

lessons.title: Sales Pipeline and Stage Ownership
lessons.description: Move leads through the pipeline with a clear purpose, complete notes, and an owner at every stage.

content.html:
What you will learn

Move leads through the pipeline with a clear purpose, complete notes, and an owner at every stage.

  • Describe the purpose of each pipeline stage
  • Identify the exit criteria for stages 1 through 4
  • Use the five-step conversation framework
  • Keep every active lead tied to a next action

Learner guide

  • A stage describes what is true now, not what you hope will happen
  • Do not advance a lead before the exit criteria are met
  • Document the reason for every stage change
  • The handoff begins only after qualification and discovery are complete

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

lessons.title: Opening the Call
lessons.description: Open seller conversations with permission, relevance, a clear frame, and a disciplined fact find.

content.html:
What you will learn

Open seller conversations with permission, relevance, a clear frame, and a disciplined fact find.

  • Use the opening frame without sounding scripted
  • Earn permission to continue
  • Capture essential property and seller facts
  • Transition from facts to deeper discovery

Learner guide

  • State why you are calling and give the seller room to respond
  • Use a calm pace and equal-status tone
  • Write down details instead of relying on memory
  • The fact find creates context. It does not replace discovery

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

lessons.title: Discovery and Handoff
lessons.description: Uncover the seller's situation, consequences, timeline, and decision process then deliver a clean acquisition handoff.

content.html:
What you will learn

Uncover the seller's situation, consequences, timeline, and decision process then deliver a clean acquisition handoff.

  • Distinguish qualification from discovery
  • Ask consequence and future-state questions
  • Confirm decision-makers and expectations
  • Complete a concise handoff with all required context

Learner guide

  • Discovery explains why the seller may act
  • Ask one question at a time and listen to the full answer
  • Do not diagnose or advise outside your role
  • A clean handoff lets the next person continue without making the seller repeat everything

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

content.description: Accessible learner guide for Discovery and Handoff

cards.front: What is the primary objective of the 'qualification' phase?

cards.back: False

cards.front: The question 'What would it mean for you to have this handled?' is used to uncover what?
cards.back: The deeper layers of seller motivation

cards.front: Why must a representative create 'safety' when asking about financial pressure?
cards.back: Because sellers are often embarrassed about financial strain and need to feel safe to reveal it

cards.front: What is the purpose of the 'what if' question in discovery?
cards.back: To force the seller to confront the consequences of doing nothing

cards.front: If a seller says they will 'lose it to the bank' if they don't sell, what does this indicate?
cards.back: They have high motivation and a high level of urgency

cards.back: Spouses; Siblings; Attorneys

modules.title: Objections and Questions
modules.description: Respond to concerns with empathy, structure, and accurate process information.

lessons.title: Objection Architecture
lessons.description: Use a repeatable framework to understand concerns before selecting a response.

content.html:
What you will learn

Use a repeatable framework to understand concerns before selecting a response.

  • Apply Listen, Acknowledge, Ask, Redirect
  • Classify common objection types
  • Avoid arguing with the seller
  • Choose a response that matches the concern

Learner guide

  • An objection is information before it is resistance
  • Acknowledge without automatically agreeing
  • Ask enough to identify the real concern
  • Redirect only after the seller feels heard

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

content.description: Accessible learner guide for Objection Architecture

cards.front: What is the primary factor that distinguishes a top-performing caller from an average one?
cards.back: Their response when a seller pushes back or provides an objection

cards.back: As a conversation that still has momentum

cards.front: How does the text define 'complaints' in the context of real estate calls?
cards.back: Frustrated venting from sellers who have likely received many investor calls

cards.front: What is the primary mistake to avoid when a seller uses a reactionary defense response like 'you've got two minutes'?
cards.back: Speeding up the process because the seller told you to hurry

cards.front: What is the primary goal of the 'Acknowledge' step in the objection framework?
cards.back: To show the seller you actually heard and understand their concern

cards.front: What is the objective of the final step, 'Redirect,' in the objection handling framework?
cards.back: To guide the conversation toward a potential solution once the concern is understood

cards.front: How should a caller respond when a seller mentions they are getting other offers?
cards.back: Encourage them to explore options and ask what they have seen so far

cards.front: How should a caller build trust with a seller who says 'I don't trust investors'?
cards.back: Mention working with licensed title companies; Mention proper closings; Mention attorney reviews

As a conversation that still has momentum
As a reason to offer a higher price

questions.question_text: How does the text define 'complaints' in the context of real estate calls?
questions.explanation: Complaints are frustrated venting from sellers who have likely received many investor calls, not genuine barriers to a deal.

options.option_text: Frustrated venting from sellers who have likely received many investor calls
Detailed objections about the property valuation

questions.question_text: What is the primary mistake to avoid when a seller uses a reactionary defense response like 'you've got two minutes'?
questions.explanation: Speeding up plays into the seller's defense mechanism. The caller should maintain a steady pace instead.

options.option_text: Speeding up the process because the seller told you to hurry
Ignoring the seller's time constraint entirely

questions.question_text: What is the primary goal of the 'Acknowledge' step in the objection framework?
questions.explanation: Acknowledging shows the seller you actually heard and understand their concern, building trust and rapport.

options.option_text: To agree with everything the seller says
To buy time to think of a rebuttal
To show the seller you actually heard and understand their concern
To repeat the seller's words back to them verbatim

questions.question_text: What is the objective of the final step, 'Redirect,' in the objection handling framework?
questions.explanation: The Redirect step guides the conversation toward a potential solution once the concern is fully understood.

options.option_text: To end the call on a positive note
To guide the conversation toward a potential solution once the concern is understood

questions.question_text: How should a caller respond when a seller mentions they are getting other offers?
questions.explanation: Encouraging exploration and asking what they've seen ensures an apples-to-apples comparison rather than seeming threatened.

options.option_text: Tell the seller other offers are likely scams
Encourage them to explore options and ask what they have seen so far
End the call and move to the next lead

questions.question_text: How should a caller build trust with a seller who says 'I don't trust investors'?
questions.explanation: Mentioning these professional safeguards demonstrates the transaction is legitimate and follows proper legal processes.

options.option_text: Mention working with licensed title companies
Offer to pay in cash upfront before closing

questions.question_text: What metaphorical description does the text use for an objection?
questions.explanation: Viewing objections as doorways rather than walls changes the caller's approach from defensive to exploratory.

options.option_text: A brick wall to break through
A doorway into a deeper conversation
A speed bump on the road to closing

questions.question_text: Acknowledging a seller's frustration about receiving too many investor calls helps de-escalate their defensive posture.
questions.explanation: Showing empathy through acknowledgment helps the seller feel heard and lowers their defenses.

questions.question_text: A caller should stay on the phone even when a seller is pushing back, because the seller is still talking and there is still momentum.
questions.explanation: As long as the seller is still engaged in conversation, there is momentum and an opportunity to move the lead forward.

questions.question_text: What is the goal of mentioning a 'licensed title company' to a skeptical seller?
questions.explanation: Mentioning a licensed title company demonstrates legitimacy and shows the transaction follows proper legal processes.

options.option_text: To impress the seller with the size of the operation
To shift liability away from the investor
To demonstrate that the transaction is legitimate and follows a legal process
To speed up the closing process

questions.question_text: When a caller uses the objection handling framework correctly, they shift from being 'pushy' to being _____.
questions.explanation: The framework transforms the caller's approach from aggressive persuasion to genuine curiosity about the seller's situation.

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of the 'Ask' phase in the framework when a seller says the price is too low?
questions.explanation: Asking about the seller's expected number determines the actual gap, which is often smaller than initially assumed.

options.option_text: To justify the current offer with comparable sales
To explain the investor's profit margins
To determine the actual gap between the offer and the seller's expectations
To delay the conversation while researching property values

questions.question_text: The 4-step framework can only be used for objections the caller has previously encountered and practiced.
questions.explanation: A primary benefit of the framework is its versatility - it can be used to handle objections the caller has never heard before.

questions.question_text: What is the first step in the framework for handling 'Your price is too low'?
questions.explanation: Even with a specific objection like price, the first step is always to listen and let the seller finish their statement.

options.option_text: Justify the offer with comparable sales data
Ask what price they had in mind
Acknowledge that the price may seem low
Listen to the seller finish their statement

questions.question_text: What is the caller's main tool for solving problems during a call?
questions.explanation: Information gathered through questions is the primary tool that enables the caller to address seller concerns effectively.

questions.question_text: What is the final goal of the 4-step objection handling framework?
questions.explanation: The ultimate purpose of the framework is to move the lead forward by resolving the seller's concerns.

options.option_text: To win the argument with the seller
To get the seller to agree on the first call
To collect as much data as possible
To move the lead forward by resolving concerns

lessons.title: Objection Scripts Playbook
lessons.description: Practice adaptable responses for price, timing, trust, competition, and decision-maker concerns.

content.html:
What you will learn

Practice adaptable responses for price, timing, trust, competition, and decision-maker concerns.

  • Select a script pattern that matches the objection
  • Adapt wording without changing its intent
  • Use questions to reopen dialogue
  • Know when to pause and seek guidance

Learner guide

  • Scripts are guardrails, not lines to recite at any cost
  • Use the seller's language when reflecting a concern
  • Do not answer a question you do not understand
  • A graceful exit protects trust when there is no fit

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

cards.front: What are the four components of the objection-handling framework taught in BMH training?

cards.back: True

cards.back: Ask what has to happen before selling makes sense, then offer a cash option, a terms option, or both

cards.back: True

cards.back: "Public records and standard outreach"

quiz.title: Objection Scripts Playbook Checkpoint
quiz.description: Each attempt draws 10 questions from the curated lesson pool.

questions.question_text: What are the four components of the objection-handling framework taught in BMH training?
questions.explanation: The framework is listen, acknowledge, ask, redirect - the foundation every objection drill builds on.

questions.question_text: When a seller says "I want more money," the playbook response is to say you're probably capped but can check for room - right after asking if they're ready to sign and move forward today.
questions.explanation: You only offer to check for more room after the closing question: "Are you ready to sign and move forward today?"

questions.question_text: A seller says they don't know their price. Which prompts does the playbook use to draw out a number? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: You ask them to imagine they did know, and use the Craigslist analogy - you wouldn't list a car for $0 and say "make me an offer."

options.option_text: "If you truly don't know, imagine you did know - what would that look like?"
The Craigslist car analogy: you wouldn't list a car for $0 and say "make me an offer"
Give them your number first so they have something to react to
End the call and tell them to call back once they've picked a price

questions.question_text: A seller says "I'm talking to other investors." What's the recommended response?
questions.explanation: You stay unbothered, ask what has to happen before selling makes sense, and offer a clean cash option, a terms option, or both.

options.option_text: Ask what has to happen before selling makes sense, then offer a cash option, a terms option, or both
Warn them that other investors can't be trusted
Drop your price immediately to beat the competition
Demand an exclusivity agreement before talking further

questions.question_text: When asked for a company name, you describe the business as a partnership that closes with a local title company so everything's transparent.
questions.explanation: The scripted answer is "we are a partnership, and we close with a local title company so everything's transparent."

questions.question_text: A seller says "I can't sell right now; I need more time." What's the goal of your response?
questions.explanation: The response takes the pressure off - no rush at all - and confirms a good time to chat again and answer questions later.

options.option_text: Take the pressure off and confirm a good time to chat again - no rush
Create urgency with an offer that expires in 24 hours
Push for a decision before the call ends
Mark them as a dead lead and move on

questions.question_text: A seller asks "How did you get my number?" What's the suggested response?
questions.explanation: The honest, simple answer is public records and standard outreach - then offer text or email going forward if they prefer.

options.option_text: Claim a mutual friend passed it along

questions.question_text: A seller says "I'll fix it myself." Which moves are part of the playbook response? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: You ask if time, cash, and contractors line up, offer to trade convenience for price, and warn about contractor roulette.

options.option_text: Ask whether time, cash, and contractors all line up
Offer to trade convenience for price if they're over budget even a little
Tell them DIY repairs always fail
Offer to manage their renovation for free

questions.question_text: A seller is running a "highest-and-best" investor open house. How do you position yourself?
questions.explanation: You opt out of the bidding circus and position yourself as the certain, no-drama lane if that route doesn't deliver.

options.option_text: As the certain, no-drama lane if the bidding route doesn't deliver
As the most aggressive bidder in the open house
As a buyer who will beat any written offer by $5,000
As a last resort who needs an answer today

questions.question_text: A seller plans to rent the property out instead. Which risks does the playbook have you raise? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: Renting can work if they're prepared for vacancies, repairs, and collections - including floating the payment when a tenant misses a month.

questions.question_text: A seller asks "If this is such a good deal, why don't you buy it yourself?" How does the playbook answer?
questions.explanation: You do buy a lot when it fits your portfolio - and when it doesn't, you keep it, partner, or place it with your team, so the seller still gets a clean, certain outcome.

options.option_text: "I do buy a lot when it fits my portfolio - when it doesn't, I keep it, partner, or place it with someone on my team"
"I never buy houses myself; I only flip contracts"
"Your house isn't good enough for my portfolio"

questions.question_text: If a deal ends up assigned to a partner, the seller's price, timeline, and certainty may change.
questions.explanation: The core promise is the opposite - regardless of the lane, the seller's price, timeline, and certainty do not change.

questions.question_text: If a seller doesn't know what they owe on the house, the title company pulls a payoff statement to get exact numbers.
questions.explanation: Title pulls the payoff so you have exact numbers, then you bring a cash option and a terms option so the seller can choose.

questions.question_text: A seller is underwater - they owe more than the house is worth. What does the terms play involve? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: You trade price for terms: take over the existing payment, cover the arrears, and structure it so the seller isn't writing a check to sell.

options.option_text: Covering arrears and structuring the rest
The seller avoids writing a check to sell
The seller brings cash to closing to cover the gap
Convincing the bank to simply erase the loan

questions.question_text: If a seller can't move out right away, the playbook says to walk away from the deal.
questions.explanation: You don't walk - you offer a leaseback: close and fund now, and they stay for an agreed period with a simple per-day use fee and a holdback.

questions.question_text: A seller wants the sale kept private. Which measures does the playbook promise? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: You keep it fully off-market: no signs, no photos online, one discreet walkthrough, and closing through a local title company.

options.option_text: A public open house on the weekend
A "SOLD" post on social media at closing

questions.question_text: Before sending a written offer, what context does the playbook say you need so you're not "shooting blind"? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: Two minutes of context - condition, timing, and what they need to walk away with - keeps you from overbidding or underbidding.

options.option_text: The net amount they need to walk away with
Every competing offer they've received in writing

questions.question_text: In the listen-acknowledge-ask-redirect framework, what is the goal of the "ask" phase?
questions.explanation: The ask phase gathers more context and narrows down the seller's true priorities before you redirect.

options.option_text: To gather more context and narrow down the seller's true priorities
To trap the seller in a contradiction
To fill silence while you plan your pitch
To stack easy yeses and build momentum

lessons.title: Complex Objections
lessons.description: Handle emotionally or structurally difficult situations with patience, boundaries, and appropriate escalation.

content.html:
What you will learn

Handle emotionally or structurally difficult situations with patience, boundaries, and appropriate escalation.

  • Recognize complex ownership and family dynamics
  • Respond to scam and privacy concerns
  • Separate empathy from legal advice
  • Escalate cases that require specialist guidance

Learner guide

  • Slow down when the situation involves grief, conflict, or financial distress
  • Never pretend to be an attorney or financial adviser
  • Confirm who can legally make decisions
  • Document sensitivities so the next team member can respond appropriately

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

lessons.title: Seller FAQ Decoder

What you will learn

Answer common seller questions through the three lenses of trust, fairness, and simplicity.

  • Answer the question that was asked before adding detail

lessons.title: Follow-Up Cadence
lessons.description: Use planned, respectful follow-up to stay present without turning persistence into pressure.

content.html:
What you will learn

Use planned, respectful follow-up to stay present without turning persistence into pressure.

  • Apply the follow-up cadence
  • Choose a relevant reason for each contact
  • Record every attempt and next action
  • Recognize when to pause or stop outreach

Learner guide

  • Every follow-up should add context or make the next step easier
  • Honor opt-outs and communication preferences
  • Use multiple approved channels when appropriate
  • Consistency matters more than one aggressive burst
